content.text:
I'm almost done with this project. Can you help me write a .gitignore for a Node project? I almost committed node_modules and my .env file has my DB password in it.

Also my README is pretty bare — what sections should a take-home README usually have?
Suggested .gitignore (node_modules, .env), .env.example template, and README structure with setup, API examples, and design notes. Helped remove an accidental duplicate docker-compose file.

Can you review my release and expire logic? Create reservation uses FOR UPDATE on the product row but I'm not sure I did the same thing correctly when releasing or when the TTL job runs.

I feel like something could go wrong if a release and a new reservation happen at the same time.

Confirmed the concern: release/expire were updating reserved_quantity without locking the product row. Recommended SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on product in those paths. Also suggested npm start/test scripts, README sections for the rubric, and auto-init via docker-entrypoint-initdb.d. User ran npm test after — 10 succeeded, 10 failed, 0 available.
I need to push this to GitHub. My username is sripriyakota2305.
Helped with git remote and commits. Push failed initially (repo didn't exist). User created repo on GitHub and pushed manually.
The assignment wants an ai-prompts folder documenting AI usage. I used Claude and Cursor. Can you help with the folder structure?
Created ai-prompts/ with prompts.md and transcript summaries. User rewrote content to reflect their actual learning process.
